findings.evidence: Roadmap lines 163-166 state the sign condition with `iteratedDeriv n f t`; grep shows only `Basic.lean:119` exposes the sign fact, and it is the within-derivative form.
findings.fix: Add a characteristic lemma such as `iteratedDeriv_nonneg_of_pos` / `neg_one_pow_mul_iteratedDeriv_nonneg` deriving `0 ≤ (-1)^n * iteratedDeriv n f t` from `hf : IsCompletelyMonotone f` and `0 < t`, using `hf.contDiffOn` and `iteratedDerivWithin_eq_iteratedDeriv`.
findings.issue: The only sign eliminator is for `iteratedDerivWithin n f (Ici 0) t`, so the public API does not provide the conventional complete-monotonicity statement for ordinary `iteratedDeriv` on the open half-line.

summary: The bundled predicate has the main projections and closure lemmas, but it does not expose the advertised bridge from within-derivatives on `[0,∞)` to ordinary iterated derivatives on `(0,∞)`. Downstream Bernstein work will otherwise have to unfold the definition and redo this conversion at every interior point.
